summary: Story 1.9 is implementation-ready for timeline filters by date/date range, tag, and location while preserving the tenant-scoped month-grouped timeline.

notes: CodeRabbit heading was normalized to match the story template.
No unfilled template placeholders were found.
Executor and quality gate are different known agents.
Quality gate tools are present and appropriate for Livewire/service/filter work.
All six PRD acceptance criteria are covered by tasks and tests.
Integration verification covers tenant scoping, eager loading, and filtered-result behavior.
Story identifies MemoryTimeline, MemoryTimelineService, memory timeline Blade view, localization files, and focused tests.
Implementation boundaries keep query logic in the service and rendering in Blade.
Scope exclusions prevent table/admin, map, masonry, advanced search, sharing, reporting, and unrelated SaaS flow changes.
Tenant scoping and cross-tenant tag/record exclusions are explicit.
Location remains transient by default and private content/media details are excluded from URL state.
Service, Livewire, route/profile regression, route-list, Pint, npm gates, structure validation, diff check, and CodeRabbit review commands are specified.
Story type, agents, quality gates, self-healing configuration, and focus areas are populated and aligned with enabled core-config.
Code intelligence helper exists but no provider is available in this session; task allows silent skip.

nice_to_have: The repo config marks PRD and architecture as sharded, but the current validation sources are the monolithic docs/prd.md and docs/architecture.md files. This did not block validation because the referenced sections are accessible there.
Story 1.8 still has a stale textual status in its story file, but the DevOps handoff reports PR #14 merged, release PASS, and tag v1.9.0.

recommended_next.reason: PO validation returned GO and Story 1.9 is now Approved for development.
